prompt: Madhubani Mithila folk painting, Bharni sub-style, on handmade paper with rice-paste white ground. Depicts a neural network as a sacred lotus vine tree with three vertical columns: left column of small closed lotus buds representing input neurons, central column of half-bloomed lotus flowers representing hidden layer neurons, right column of one large fully open lotus in full bloom representing the output neuron. All columns connected by curling organic vines and stems carrying small fish and peacocks symbolizing signal flow. Bold double outlines on every form: thick outer black line and thin inner black line in lampblack ink. Flat solid unmodulated color fill — vermilion red, turmeric yellow, indigo blue, palasha orange, bilva leaf green. Every background gap filled with horror vacui micro-patterns: tiny fish, geometric cross-hatching, dot clusters, small flowers. Slight organic irregularity in symmetry mimicking hand-painted quality. Sun symbol upper left, moon symbol upper right. Enclosed by three layered decorative border bands with repeating chevron, dot, and floral geometric patterns. No text, no labels, no typography anywhere. Natural pigment palette only. Strict 2D flat perspective, no depth, no shading. (1024*1024px)
tradition_period: Origins ~7th–8th century BCE, Bihar, India; GI tag 2007; modern documented tradition 1960s–present
tradition_approach: Bharni sub-style: double outlines (thick outer, thin inner in lampblack), flat unmodulated natural pigment fills, horror vacui space-filling, lotus bud-to-bloom progression encoding network layers, fish and vine connections, layered geometric border bands, strict 2D flat perspective.
